title: Prenatal exposure to arsenic and cadmium impacts infectious disease-related genes within the glucocorticoid receptor signal transduction pathway.
ehr_evidence: No mention of EHR or linked health data in the provided text.
summary: A compilation of research titles exploring the impacts of prenatal and early-life arsenic and cadmium exposure on infant health, including birth weight, immune system development, and epigenetic modifications.
key_findings: Arsenic exposure is associated with lower birth weight and increased risk of respiratory infections.
Cadmium exposure is linked to altered thymus and spleen cell phenotypes.
Prenatal arsenic exposure is associated with altered DNA methylation in cord blood.
study_design: Prospective cohort studies and laboratory experiments
population: Infants, newborns, and pregnant women in Bangladesh, USA, Chile, and India
limitations: The input text is a list of titles rather than a single cohesive manuscript, making it difficult to extract specific methodology.
